LAW AND MOTION 302, NO OTHER APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ALLEGED A SUFFICIENT BASIS FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES OR RESTITUTION UNDER THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW. AS TO FEES, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN THIS ACTION VINDICATES AN IMPORTANT PUBLIC RIGHT, CONFERS A SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT ON THE GENERAL PUBLIC OR IMPOSES A FINANCIAL BURDEN ON PLAINTIFFS WHICH IS OUT OF PROPORTION TO THEIR INDIVIDUAL STAKE. AS TO RESTITUTION, NEITHER DEFENDANT IS IN POSSESSION OF FUNDS OR HAS BENEFITED FROM ANY PROPERTY GAINED THROUGH ANY ALLEGED UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Defendant Bank Of America, N.A. Motion To Strike 3rd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicholas Short, Esq., of Winston & Strawn LLP, Tel. 415.591.6856, appearing for Defendant and moving party

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Bank Of America, N.A.'s Motion To Strike Portions of the 3rd Amended Complaint is Granted. Plaintiffs have not alleged a sufficient basis for attorney's fees or restitution under the Unfair Competition Law. As to fees, Plaintiffs have not shown this action vindicates an important public right, confers a significant benefit on the general public or imposes a financial burden on plaintiffs which is out of proportion to their individual stake. As to restitution, neither Defendant is in possession of funds or has benefited from any property gained through any alleged unfair business practice. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
